fullArticle:
When Sen. Elizabeth Warren first heard that Donald Trump was refusing to sign a landmark housing-affordability bill that she had stewarded through the Senate — with virtually universal bipartisan support — she thought it was a “joke.”

Last Wednesday, inside the Capitol’s Statuary Hall, a carpeted red stage with a small writing desk had already been arranged for the planned signing ceremony. To its left, a podium affixed with the presidential seal and 11 artfully draped flags framed the scene. But roughly two hours before the bill was set to become law, Trump pulled the rug out from not only Democrats, but also his own party, canceling the ceremony and signaling that he would not sign the ROAD to Housing Act until Congress passed his pet voter-suppression law, the “SAVE America” Act.
